rationale: US NATO asset cuts remain the dominant concern for Romania's Black Sea flank; Iran deal progress offers mild energy price relief but no structural change this cycle.

top_events.title: US to cut air and naval assets deployed for NATO operations in Europe
top_events.summary: Reduced US naval and air presence directly affects Romania's Black Sea security posture. No new announcement this cycle but the confirmed cuts remain a live concern.

top_events.title: Iran says deal to end fighting with US has 'never been closer'
top_events.summary: Progress toward a US-Iran deal would lower energy prices, offering indirect cost-of-living relief for Romanian households. No signing or timeline yet.

top_events.title: Putin admits Ukraine attacks hitting Russian economy and society
top_events.summary: Rare acknowledgment of war's domestic toll on Russia is a marginal positive signal for regional security near Romania's eastern border, though no immediate change.
